title: C001: Add Keystone container image and release config
summary: Review #8 covers the changes addressing greptile-apps[bot] review #7 feedback. Four targeted fixes were applied: (1) DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive added to apt-get RUN instructions in images/keystone/Dockerfile and images/venv-builder/Dockerfile, matching the existing pattern in images/python-base/Dockerfile; (2) inline if/grep/PASS/FAIL block in verify_keystone.sh replaced with the shared assert_contains helper; (3) case-insensitive sed matching (/Id flag) added to both sed deletion patterns in apply-constraint-overrides.sh to handle PyPI's case-insensitive package names; (4) new Test 7 added to test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh covering the case-mismatch scenario.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (7 tests, 20 assertions, 0 failures)
Edge cases are covered (case-insensitive matching, missing files, comments/blanks)
New test covers the specific bug being fixed (Test 7: case-mismatch)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (DEBIAN_FRONTEND consistent across all 3 Dockerfiles)
Uses shared assertion helpers consistently (assert_contains replaces inline logic)
Changes are minimal and tightly scoped to reviewer feedback

security_checklist.item: Case-insensitive matching prevents silent duplicate constraints (supply chain safety)
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive prevents interactive prompt stalls in CI

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (single-flag additions, helper reuse)
Dockerfile pattern is consistent across all three images

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (inline assertion replaced with shared helper)
No unnecessary additions beyond reviewer requests

fail_fast_checklist.item: Script fails early on missing constraints file (existing behavior preserved)

defensive_checklist.item: Package name dot-escaping in sed patterns prevents false matches
Case-insensitive matching prevents silent constraint duplication
